questions.prompt: Why is roofing and weatherproofing a schedule-critical milestone on a residential project?
choices: It establishes dry-in so moisture risk is reduced before interior work
It allows final accounting closeout to begin
It completes all permit inspections automatically
It eliminates the need for trade coordination
questions.explanation: Dry-in is a major sequencing gate: once the roof assembly is weather-protective, interior work can proceed with lower exposure to rain-related damage and rework.

questions.prompt: Before relying on remembered roof-detail rules in Oregon, what should a contractor do first?
choices: Verify the current adopted code cycle and local jurisdiction requirements
Proceed with installation and update details at inspection
Use only supplier guidance without code cross-check
Wait until interior punch list to confirm details
questions.explanation: Oregon code cycles and local implementation details can change; validating currently adopted requirements first prevents avoidable corrections and failed inspections.

questions.prompt: Which field condition is a common roofing edge case that requires strong GC coordination?
choices: Late MEP penetrations after roofing is complete
questions.explanation: Late penetrations can compromise completed weatherproofing unless detailing, resealing, and reinspection are coordinated immediately and documented.
